node.title: JSP Nitric Acid Substitute Sodium Nitrate for Gold Refining & Recovery - Reagent Grade (4 oz, 8 oz , & 16 oz bag)
node.description: JSP Nitric Acid Substitute is designed to dissolve and refine gold when combined with hydrochloric acid, facilitating the precipitation and recovery of precious metals. Directions for Use: Prepare the Solution: Dissolve 80 grams (2.8 oz) of JSP Nitric Acid Substitute in 50 milliliters (1.7 fl oz) of distilled water. Use a small glass mixing container, adding the water all at once and swirling to aid dissolution. Note: Distilled water is recommended to prevent impurities from affecting the solution's composition. Add Hydrochloric Acid: Measure 100 milliliters (3.4 fl oz) of JSP Hydrochloric Acid using a graduated cylinder for accuracy. Carefully transfer the acid into the prepared solution, avoiding spills or splashes. Gently swirl the container to mix the components thoroughly. Caution: Perform this step in a well-ventilated area or under a fume hood, as the reaction may emit strong fumes. Set Up a Safety Containment: Fill a larger container with 50 milliliters (1.7 fl oz) of water to create a secondary containment. Place the mixing container into this larger container, ensuring at least 1/4 inch (0.64 cm) of water surrounds it to catch any potential overflow. Introduce Copper: Weigh out at least 32 grams (1.1 oz) of laboratory-grade 99% pure copper pellets. Add the copper to the acid mixture in the mixing container. Note: Copper initiates the oxidation reaction necessary for producing nitric acid. Cover and React: Cover the mixing container with a larger glass container to trap nitrogen dioxide gas produced during the reaction. Allow the reaction to proceed for approximately 1 hour, during which the gas will bubble through the water, forming nitric acid. Tip: Place a weight on the covering container to prevent it from lifting due to gas pressure. Safety Precautions: Always wear gloves and safety goggles when handling acids, as hydrochloric acid is highly corrosive and can cause severe burns upon contact with skin. Handle all chemicals with care, adhering to standard laboratory safety protocols. By following these steps, you can effectively use JSP Nitric Acid Substitute in conjunction with hydrochloric acid to dissolve and refine gold, facilitating its subsequent precipitation and recovery. Nitric acid substitute to be mixed with hydrochloric acid. This is used to dissolve and refine gold for later precipitation and recovery. Directions for use: Mix 2 tablespoons for each ounce of metal. 2 tablespoons per 90ml of hydrochloric acid. Hydrochloric acid can be found here: Hydrochloric Acid 8 oz Bottle You choose the size!

node.title: JSP 32oz Nitric Acid 69.8% Concentrated ACS Lab Grade Best for Gold Refining
node.description: ⚠️ Important Compliance & Safety Notice This listing is for Nitric Acid (HNO₃), 69.8% ACS Reagent Grade, 32 oz (1000 mL). Nitric acid is a regulated, corrosive, and oxidizing substance. Age Requirement: Must be 21+ years of age to purchase. Intended Use: For laboratory, industrial, jewelry refining, and research applications only. Not for household use. Legal Responsibility: By purchasing, you agree to comply with all local, state, and federal laws regarding purchase, possession, and use. Buyers assume full responsibility for safe handling and proper use. Shipping Restrictions: Ships via UPS Ground to the lower 48 U.S. states only. Cannot ship to PO Boxes, APO/FPO, international addresses, or freight forwarders. Return Policy: This item cannot be returned once shipped. Safety Information: Always wear PPE (acid-resistant gloves, goggles, face shield, protective clothing). Use only in a well-ventilated area or under a fume hood. SDS available upon request. Product Details Grade: ACS Reagent / Lab/Technical Grade Purity: 69.8% v/v Volume: 32 oz (1000 mL) Molecular Formula: HNO₃ Formula Weight: 63.013 CAS Number: 7697-37-2 Synonyms: Aqua Fortis, Hydrogen Nitrate, Salpetersäure Packaging: Supplied in a DOT-approved, acid-resistant polymer safety bottle. These specialized containers are rigid, non-reactive, leak-resistant, and far safer than ordinary HDPE. Common Applications Precious metal refining (gold, silver, platinum, palladium) Preparation of Aqua Regia (when combined with hydrochloric acid) Industrial chemical processing Laboratory and research use (not for food, drug, or medical use) Safety & Handling Hazards: Highly corrosive, strong oxidizer, produces toxic vapors. Storage: Store below 75°F in original container, away from incompatible materials (bases, organics, metals). Dilution Rule: Always add acid to water, never water to acid. First Aid: Eyes: Flush with water for 15 minutes, seek immediate medical help. Skin: Rinse with water for 15 minutes, remove contaminated clothing. Inhalation: Move to fresh air, seek immediate medical attention. Ingestion: Do not induce vomiting. Call poison control immediately. 📞 Emergency Contact (24/7): CHEMTREC 1-800-424-9300📄 Please review the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) before use New to gold refining? These guides are designed to help you better understand refining processes and the tools commonly used in precious metal work. Explore the Learning Center HERE

node.title: Nitric Acid 70% for Gold Refining – ACS Reagent Grade 32oz -Higher Purity Concentrated Acid for Gold Refining & Recovery
node.description: To celebrate the launch we are offering 10% off for a limited time. GTE Nitric Acid – 69.8–70% ACS Reagent Grade Professional Nitric Acid for Gold Refining, Aqua Regia Preparation & Precious Metal Recovery GTE Nitric Acid (HNO₃) 69.8–70% ACS Reagent Grade is a high-purity laboratory chemical widely used for gold refining, aqua regia preparation, precious metal recovery, and industrial laboratory applications. Nitric acid plays a critical role in gold refining chemistry. When combined with hydrochloric acid, it forms aqua regia, the classic solution used by refiners to dissolve gold and other precious metals during the refining and recovery process. Reliable reagent-grade purity and consistent concentration make this nitric acid suitable for refiners, jewelers, laboratories, and precious metal recovery professionals who require dependable chemical performance. Nitric Acid Comparison JSP Nitric AcidConcentration: 69.8% ACS Reagent GradeManufacturer: JSP ChemicalReputation: Trusted industry standard since 1983Best for: Jewelers, pawn shops, and refiners who prefer the original JSP formulation vs GTE Nitric AcidConcentration: 69.8–70% ACS Reagent GradeBrand: GTE Gold & Silver Testing EquipmentAdvantage: Produced at the upper end of the ACS purity rangeBest for: Refiners who want maximum strength and performance for aqua regia and gold recovery Both products are fresh, laboratory grade nitric acid suitable for gold refining and precious metal recovery. Comprehensive Benefits and Uses of Nitric Acid 70% Nitric acid is one of the most important gold refining chemicals used by refiners worldwide. It is commonly used to remove base metals, prepare aqua regia solutions, and assist in dissolving precious metals during the refining process. When nitric acid is mixed with hydrochloric acid in the proper ratio, the resulting solution—aqua regia—is capable of dissolving gold and other precious metals that resist most other acids. High-purity nitric acid helps ensure consistent reactions during gold refining and precious metal recovery, making it an essential chemical for refining operations. ⚠ Important Compliance & Safety Notice Nitric Acid (HNO₃) is a regulated oxidizing and corrosive chemical and must only be handled by individuals trained in chemical safety procedures. Age Requirement Must be 21 years of age or older to purchase. Intended Use For gold refining, aqua regia preparation, precious metal recovery, laboratory use, research, and industrial chemical applications only.Not intended for household use. Legal Responsibility By purchasing this product, the buyer agrees to comply with all applicable local, state, and federal regulations governing the purchase, storage, and handling of nitric acid. The buyer assumes full responsibility for safe handling and lawful use. 🚛 Shipping & Return Policy Due to hazardous material shipping regulations: • Ships UPS Ground only• Available to the Lower 48 United States• Cannot ship to PO Boxes, APO/FPO addresses, Alaska, Hawaii, or international destinations• No returns accepted once product has shipped Nitric acid is packaged in DOT-compliant, acid-resistant safety bottles designed for safe storage and shipment under Limited Quantity ground transport rules. ⚗ Product Details Chemical Name: Nitric AcidChemical Formula: HNO₃CAS Number: 7697-37-2 Grade: ACS Reagent / Laboratory GradePurity: 69.8–70% v/vVolume: 32 oz (1000 mL) Common Synonyms:Aqua FortisHydrogen NitrateSalpetersäure This reagent-grade nitric acid is commonly used in precious metal refining, gold recovery processes, laboratory chemistry, and industrial chemical applications. ⚙ Common Applications This nitric acid is commonly used for: • Gold refining and precious metal recovery• Preparing aqua regia for dissolving gold• Refining scrap gold jewelry and electronic scrap• Jewelry testing and metal analysis• Laboratory and research chemistry• Industrial chemical processes 🧪 Safety & Handling Guidelines Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) Always wear appropriate protective equipment when handling nitric acid: • Acid-resistant gloves• Chemical splash goggles• Protective clothing• Face shield when appropriate Use only in a well-ventilated area or under a chemical fume hood. Storage Store below 75°F (24°C) in the original sealed container. Keep away from: • organic materials• bases or alkaline substances• metals• incompatible or flammable chemicals Dilution Rule When diluting nitric acid: Always add acid to water — never water to acid. This helps prevent violent reactions or dangerous splashing. 🆘 First Aid Measures Eye Contact: Flush immediately with water for at least 15 minutes and seek medical attention. Skin Contact: Remove contaminated clothing and rinse skin with large amounts of water for 15 minutes. Inhalation: Move the affected person to fresh air and seek medical assistance if symptoms persist. Ingestion: Do not induce vomiting. Contact Poison Control or seek immediate medical attention. 📞 Emergency Contact CHEMTREC (24-Hour Emergency Response)1-800-424-9300 Safety Data Sheet (SDS) available upon request. 🏭 Manufactured for GTE Gold & Silver Testing Equipmentwww.goldandsilvertesting.com Made in the USA New to gold refining? Visit our Gold Refining Learning Center for beginner-friendly guides that explain the basics of gold recovery, refining concepts, common materials found in electronics, and how precious metals are identified and evaluated. These guides are designed to help you better understand refining processes and the tools commonly used in precious metal work. Explore the Learning Center HERE High Purity and Quality Assurance GTE Nitric Acid 70% is a concentrated acid solution formulated to meet ACS reagent grade standards. This high level of purity ensures reliable and consistent results during usage, particularly important in sensitive chemical applications such as gold refining. The 69.8-70% nitric acid concentration guarantees optimal oxidation potential and minimal impurities, elevating performance for precise laboratory or industrial processes. Choosing a high-purity nitric acid helps maintain chemical integrity and efficiency during refining operations and prevents contamination that could compromise metal recovery yield. Optimized for Gold Refining and Metal Recovery This Nitric Acid 70% concentration is specifically suited for gold refining and recovery applications, where its oxidizing properties efficiently dissolve metal impurities. The acid assists in separating gold from other metals, making it essential for collectors, jewelers, and small-scale refinery operations. Its concentrated nature helps accelerate the refining process, reducing time and chemical consumption, while maintaining environmental safety when handled properly. This product empowers users with a dependable reagent suited for expert-level gold purification tasks. Safe Handling and Storage Convenience GTE Nitric Acid 70% is packaged in a 32oz container that balances ease of handling and storage safety. The container is designed to resist corrosion, ensuring the acid remains stable and uncontaminated during storage. Proper labeling and secure sealing reduce the risk of accidental spills or exposure, making it suitable for laboratory and workshop environments. Following recommended safety guidelines, including wearing protective gear and storing in a cool, ventilated area, ensures that users benefit fully while maintaining a safe workspace.

node.title: Fume Extractor for Gold Refining & Acid Work – Removes Nitric & Aqua Regia Fumes at Source
node.description: Protect your workspace and your lungs with this high-performance fume extractor, designed to capture and filter harmful fumes, dust, and airborne particles at the source. Whether you're soldering, refining, working with chemicals, or doing detailed bench work, this system pulls contaminants away from your breathing zone and delivers cleaner, safer air in your workspace. Why You Need This If you're working with acids, soldering, or heating metals, you're producing fumes whether you see them or not. This unit is built to capture those fumes immediately, not after they’ve already filled your space. Key Features Multi-stage filtration system captures fumes, dust, and fine particles Removes harmful vapors and airborne contaminants at the source Flexible extraction arm allows precise positioning exactly where you need it High airflow performance for fast and effective air cleaning Compact, portable design fits easily into workshops and bench setups Quiet operation for comfortable daily use Built For Jewelry soldering and metalsmithing Gold and silver refining Acid and chemical work Bench work and repair stations Small workshops and production environments Filtration System Equipped with a 3-stage filtration setup, including pre-filters and advanced filtration layers to capture both visible dust and microscopic airborne particles . Specifications Power: 210W Noise Level: <65 dB Filtration: 3-stage system Weight: ~14.5 kg Compact footprint for bench or floor use What Makes This Different Most people rely on fans or open windows, which just move fumes around. This system actively captures and filters contaminants, giving you real protection instead of a false sense of ventilation. Bottom Line If you're serious about your work, you need to be serious about your air quality. This is one of those upgrades that pays for itself in comfort, safety, and long-term health.
